plan_adherence_notes: All 5 planned steps executed faithfully. Step 1: AssumptionContext.test.tsx (9 tests, 83 lines vs 90 estimated) covers add/dedup/remove/clearAll/has/get/contextPayload/throw-without-provider. Step 2: SessionContext.test.tsx (9 tests, 71 lines vs 70 estimated) covers defaults/localStorage persistence/setAccountId/setAgentId/corrupt data/partial data/isConfigured/throw-without-provider. Step 3: ResolveMarketPanel.test.tsx (12 tests, 176 lines vs 150 estimated) covers all 3 render states, two-step confirmation flow, cancel, reset on selection change, mutation success/failure, closed market. Step 4: ConditionalEditor.test.tsx (11 tests, 252 lines vs 180 estimated) covers session guard, form rendering, unconditional/conditional modes, add/remove context rows, disabled/enabled outcome select, submit payload, success/error. Step 5: VariableRow.test.tsx (14 tests, 277 lines vs 150 estimated) covers null state, display, assume toggle, session-dependent edit buttons, inline edit slider, cancel, submit payload, contextPayload integration, success/error, target market label. All tests mock at API client level per decision d2. All files in src/__tests__/ per decision d5. AssumptionProvider wrapped inline per decision d5. Total: 55 tests, ~859 lines (vs 640 estimated). Slightly exceeded estimates on component tests due to more thorough coverage, which is a positive outcome.
missed_questions: What edge cases exist around concurrent assumption modifications across multiple VariableRow instances sharing the same AssumptionProvider?
Should probability distribution resolution (resolutionProbabilities) rendering in ResolveMarketPanel be tested for edge cases like 0% or 100% outcomes?
reason: All 5 planned test files were created covering the 5 highest-priority untested components. 55 tests pass cleanly (1.93s). Mocking patterns are consistent with existing codebase conventions (vi.mock at API client level, renderWithProviders helper). Test coverage addresses the key behaviors identified in research: mutation flows, state management operations, form interactions, session-dependent UI, and error handling. The plan correctly deprioritized graph visualization and hooks.ts per research findings. Minor line count overruns are positive (more thorough coverage). No production code was modified. The work measurably improves frontend test coverage for the most business-critical untested components.
